citations.title: Area-selective deposition: aniline inhibitor with BDEAS precursor on amine -NH sites
citations.abstract: This synthetic abstract reports that the inhibitor aniline passivates the amine -NH sites via chemisorption on the non-growth surface, while the BDEAS precursor grows the target film on the growth surface. Selectivity is driven by chemisorption on the non-growth surface; measured selectivity approaches the target at 10 nm oxide thickness.

citations.title: Area-selective deposition: pivalic acid inhibitor with TDMAT precursor on a-SiN non-growth surface
citations.abstract: This synthetic abstract reports that the inhibitor pivalic acid passivates the a-SiN non-growth surface via differential adsorption energy, while the TDMAT precursor grows the target film on the growth surface. Selectivity is driven by differential adsorption energy; measured selectivity approaches the target at 10 nm oxide thickness.

citations.title: Area-selective deposition: methanesulfonic acid inhibitor with BDEAS precursor on amine -NH sites
citations.abstract: This synthetic abstract reports that the inhibitor methanesulfonic acid passivates the amine -NH sites via nucleation delay, while the BDEAS precursor grows the target film on the growth surface. Selectivity is driven by nucleation delay; measured selectivity approaches the target at 10 nm oxide thickness.

citations.title: Area-selective deposition: DMATMS inhibitor with TDMAT precursor on a-SiO2 growth surface
citations.abstract: This synthetic abstract reports that the inhibitor DMATMS passivates the a-SiO2 growth surface via differential adsorption energy, while the TDMAT precursor grows the target film on the growth surface. Selectivity is driven by differential adsorption energy; measured selectivity approaches the target at 10 nm oxide thickness.

citations.title: Area-selective deposition: methanesulfonic acid inhibitor with TMA precursor on silanol sites
citations.abstract: This synthetic abstract reports that the inhibitor methanesulfonic acid passivates the silanol sites via chemisorption on the non-growth surface, while the TMA precursor grows the target film on the growth surface. Selectivity is driven by chemisorption on the non-growth surface; measured selectivity approaches the target at 10 nm oxide thickness.

citations.title: Area-selective deposition: fundamentals, applications, and future outlook
citations.abstract: Review of area-selective deposition. Selectivity arises from dosing a molecular inhibitor that chemisorbs on the non-growth surface and blocks the ALD precursor there, while the growth surface stays reactive. The film nucleates on the growth surface and is delayed on the non-growth surface; that nucleation delay is the selectivity.

citations.title: Computational investigation of precursor blocking by an aniline small-molecule inhibitor
citations.abstract: Aniline chemisorbs on Ru/Co non-growth areas but only physisorbs on the SiO2 growth area. DFT adsorption energies plus random sequential adsorption coverage explain the selective blocking: strong chemisorption on the non-growth surface, weak physisorption on the growth surface.

citations.title: Area-selective ALD of Al2O3 using a methanesulfonic acid inhibitor
citations.abstract: Methanesulfonic acid shows reaction barriers on SiO2 an order of magnitude higher than on Cu, giving >97% selective Al2O3 growth. Selectivity is dominated by the differential precursor half-reaction barrier.

citations.title: Ground-up generation of periodic slab models of dehydroxylated amorphous silica
citations.abstract: Dehydroxylated silica converges to ~1.15 OH/nm2 (Zhuravlev), with controlled protocols in the 0.35-2.0 OH/nm2 band. Computed reactivity is highly sensitive to the presumed surface site density, so slab models must be gated against experimental silanol densities.

citations.title: Amorphous silica slab models with variable roughness and silanol density
citations.abstract: Melt-cleave-quench-functionalize protocol builds amorphous silica slabs with controlled roughness and silanol density using the BKS potential.

citations.title: MAD-SURF: fine-tuning MACE-MPA-0 for molecular adsorption on surfaces
citations.abstract: Fine-tuning MACE-MPA-0 markedly improves adsorption geometry fidelity for molecules on surfaces; foundation MLIPs estimate adsorption energies at a fraction of DFT cost.

citations.title: Fine-tuning foundation MLIPs with frozen transfer learning
citations.abstract: Foundation MLIPs systematically underestimate reaction barriers (minimum-energy-path heights). Absolute barriers should be treated as lower bounds and calibrated against literature DFT.

hypotheses.statement: A precursor-based small-molecule inhibitor selectively passivates the non-growth surface while surface grows the target film on the growth surface, achieving >=90% selectivity at 10 nm for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: Derived from co-occurrence of precursor and surface in the knowledge graph.

supporting_evidence.statement: Literature links precursor to passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

contradicting_evidence.statement: Some sources do not isolate precursor's specific effect.

assumptions: precursor is measurable in the relevant system.

hypotheses.statement: Chemisorption of surface on the non-growth surface blocks growth precursor adsorption, delaying nucleation enough to reach the selectivity target for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
